issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open/close/toggle functions change the visual state of the drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation menu is expanded or collapsed, breaking WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle (switch) does not update aria-checked

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing `aria‑expanded` and not initialised on page load

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing `aria‑expanded` and not updated on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: In **dropdownMenu.register** the trigger button gets `aria‑haspopup` but never gets `aria‑expanded`. The open/close helpers (`_openInstance`, `_closeInstance`) also do not toggle this attribute, leaving assistive technologies unaware of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle element missing `aria‑pressed` and not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period switch (`_toggleEl` in **pricing**) is given `role="switch"` but never receives an `aria‑pressed` state, nor is it updated when the period changes. Users cannot tell whether the switch is on (annual) or off (monthly).

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: **inlineValidator** marks fields as invalid only by adding CSS classes and error messages. It never adds `aria-invalid="true"` on the field, so screen readers do not receive the error state.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear `aria‑invalid` when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field is corrected, the validator removes the error message but leaves `aria-invalid="true"` set, causing a persistent false‑positive error state for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
(combined with Issue 4) ensure the attribute is set to `"false"` (or removed) on the valid path, e.g.:  
```js
if (result.valid) {
  removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid'); // or set to "false"
}
```

fpTitles: Dropdown menu Escape‑key handler does not update `aria‑expanded` when closing via keyboard

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function opens / closes the off‑canvas drawer but never sets `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` the panel visibility is toggled, but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never changed, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel but does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the question button, nor does `close()` set it to `"false"`. This violates the accordion accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period UI (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` but never receives an `aria‑pressed` value when the user switches between monthly/annual plans, so assistive tech cannot convey the current state.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter results are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters()` updates the visible card count (`_updateCountBadge`) but never announces the new total to screen readers, leaving users unaware of filter results.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` changes the displayed prices and savings badges but does not provide an audible cue for the change (e.g., “Annual billing selected”).

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` marks fields with CSS classes (`invalidClass`) but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on valid input). Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect validation errors.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded is not initialised on page load for the mobile‑nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger button (`_trigger`) is never given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute during initialization, so its state is undefined for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init()` after obtaining `_trigger`, add:  
```js
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded is not initialised on page load for dropdown triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are created with `aria-haspopup="true"` but lack an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, leaving their collapsed state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix:
In `dropdownMenu.register()` after setting `aria-haspopup`, also set:  
```js
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox trigger for the suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is never toggled when the listbox opens or closes. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: Line (siteSearch._open): insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line (siteSearch._close): insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the aria-invalid attribute is not set to "true". Screen readers rely on this attribute to announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line (srUtils.markInvalid): insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation clears an error from a field, the aria-invalid attribute is not removed or set to "false", causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line (srUtils.markValid): insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` at the end of the function after updating aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button is created in a closed state, but aria-expanded="false" is not set on page load. Screen readers may assume the expanded state is unknown.
issuesFound.fix: Line (mobileNav.init): insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce changes
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count and filter state are not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users navigating with assistive technologies are not informed that the product grid has updated or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line (productFilter.applyFilters): insert `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.filterResultsCount || '{n} products found').replace('{n}', String(totalMatches)));` immediately after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` inside the filterAnimation callback.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, the price amounts update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technologies are not informed that prices have changed or what the new amounts are.
issuesFound.fix: Line (pricing.setPeriod): insert `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices switched to {period}').replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));` immediately after the forEach loop that updates plan cards.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field fails validation, the function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the function removes the error description but leaves aria-invalid="true" (or doesn't explicitly set it to "false"), causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the visible card count updates in the DOM, but the change is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed how many products match the selected filter.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually, but the change is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance() and _closeInstance(), but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. The register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but fails to initialise aria-expanded="false". Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2620 (inside _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2634 (inside _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`. Line 2720 (inside register function): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on question triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion opens and closes answer panels via the open() and close() functions, but neither updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (item.trigger). While the init() function sets the initial state, the dynamic state changes during user interaction are not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4160 (inside open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`. Line 4182 (inside close function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The site search implements a combobox pattern but fails to toggle aria-expanded on the input element when the suggestion list opens and closes. The _open() and _close() functions manage the open state internally but do not reflect this state in the ARIA attribute. The init() function also omits the initial aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 3360 (inside _open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line 3372 (inside _close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`. Line 3494 (inside init): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after mounting the listbox.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (_periodBtns) act as two-state toggles (month/quarter/year) but the _syncToggleState() function only toggles CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed to indicate which period is currently active, preventing screen reader users from identifying the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3780 (inside _syncToggleState): Replace the forEach block with: `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); });`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid and markValid do not manage aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation utility functions markInvalid() and markValid() link error messages via aria-describedby but fail to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot identify which fields contain errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1770 (inside markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`. Line 1782 (inside markValid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after removing the describedby reference.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function visually updates all price displays when switching between monthly, quarterly, and annual billing, but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users receive no auditory confirmation that the billing period has changed or what the new prices are.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-selected (or aria-pressed)
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons ([data-filter-value]) function as a single-select tablist or toggle button group, but _syncTabAriaStates() only toggles CSS classes. The buttons lack aria-selected (for tabs) or aria-pressed (for toggle buttons), so screen readers cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3894 (inside _syncTabAriaStates): Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` or `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after determining isActive.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus management and focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, and when it closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Additionally, the createFocusTrap instance (_trap) is never activated in open() or deactivated in close(), allowing focus to escape to background elements while the drawer is open. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2454 (inside open function): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` and `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus();`. Line 2474 (inside close function): Ensure `_trap.deactivate();` is called (currently conditional on _trap.isActive() but _trap is never set).

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard personal data inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form fields (wizard_name, wizard_email, wizard_card_name) collect personal information but the validation binding code does not ensure autocomplete attributes are present. The fields are identified by name selectors containing personal data signals ("name", "email", "card") but lack programmatically set autocomplete values (e.g., "name", "email", "cc-name").
issuesFound.fix: Line 4430 (inside _validateCurrentStep or during binding in init): For each field, add autocomplete attribute based on field name: if field.name includes 'name' add `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'name');`, if 'email' add `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'email');`, if 'card' add `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'cc-name');`.
